text: Al-Najwa Verse was abrogated by its subsequent verse (the verse 13 of Quran 58), Lo 05 a3 a1 1525 B30 15375 B 12808 (K A ol ki o 3° B35 (ot 5 5 o0 3 of 22l el Were you apprehensive of offering charities before your secret talks? So, as you did not do it, and Allah was clement to you, maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and obey Allah and His Apostle. And Allah is well aware of what you do. After the legislation of the ruling of paying charity prior to private consultation with the Prophet (s), no Musiim acted upon it except ‘Al (a).8] Thus, in the subsequent verse, Muslims who refused to pay the charity from the fear of poverty were strongly reprehended, and then the ruling of the obligation of paying such charity was cancelled, and their sins in the disobedience of the command was forgiven, and instead, the verse emphasizes on acting upon ather obligations, avoiding forbidden actions, and obeying God and His Prophet (s).[%
